markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Mark moved/deleted sources stale without deleting history or breaking citations. This MT belongs to SourceIngestionAndEvidence. Ingest project, governance, media, and research sources with evidence and failure receipts.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Source ingestion must be allowlisted, hash-based, restartable, and secret-aware.
PDF/media extraction failures must produce receipts and repairable metadata rather than silently indexing partial content.
scope.expected_behavior: DeletedMovedSourceHandling leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: DeletedMovedSourceHandling is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 58b2af10; real-PG tests green (group total 80 passed/0 failed; lopdf 0.41 MIT only dep added). Pending adversarial code review before READY_FOR_VALIDATION.

concerns: NO HARD DELETE confirmed (spec: authority is never hard-deleted). mark_knowledge_source_stale only sets stale=TRUE; there is NO `DELETE FROM knowledge_sources` anywhere in the ingestion engine or store. A moved file marks the OLD path-row stale and the NEW path gets a fresh source row (new source_id, same content_hash) — history + receipts preserved and still citable. Verified by lifecycle mt093 (old row still exists, stale=true, receipts non-empty; stale-mark is EventLedger-backed; re-pass does not re-mark = idempotent).

STABLE-ID nuance: source_id is stable PER PATH (upsert ON CONFLICT (root_id, relative_path) keeps the existing row's id across re-index). A MOVE does NOT carry the id to the new path — the new path is a NEW source_id. So citations to the old source_id keep resolving (stale row retained) and the new path has its own id; there is no id continuity across a move. This matches 'mark stale without breaking citations' but means a citation does not auto-follow a move.

MOVED-vs-DELETED disposition heuristic is content-hash equality (engine.rs:636): if two DISTINCT files share identical content and one is deleted, the deleted one is mislabeled 'moved' (pointing at the surviving duplicate). Disposition-accuracy bug only — no authority loss, the row is still correctly marked stale with a (possibly wrong) moved_to hint.
MINOR: moved_to picks the FIRST outcome with matching content+different id; with N identical-content files the target is arbitrary among them.
evasion_probes: move old_name.md->new_name.md: old row stale, disposition=moved, moved_to=new_name.md, ledger event source_stale_marked CONFIRMED.
re-pass with no changes: stale rows NOT re-marked (idempotent) CONFIRMED.
old source row + its receipts survive (no hard delete, history citable) CONFIRMED.
already-stale sources skipped in the detection loop (source.stale check) CONFIRMED.
sources with NULL relative_path skipped (non-file sources) CONFIRMED.
evidence_checked: storage/knowledge.rs:3060 mark_knowledge_source_stale (stale=TRUE only, no delete); :2966 upsert ON CONFLICT keeps source_id

evidence: Adversarial code review PASS/PASS_WITH_NOTES (ADVERSARIAL_CODE_REVIEWER-20260611-PM); only LOW non-blocking notes.

findings.required_remediation: Do not resubmit MT-093 until MT-081 is READY_FOR_VALIDATION and passes WP Validator review, or update the dependency graph through the packet authority if the dependency is no longer real.

Dependency failures use deterministic packet status/dependency reads only; WIP MT work products were not inspected.

remediation_v2.fail_verdict: FAIL_V1 BLOCKED_ON_DEPENDENCY (MT-081 unfinished at validation time)
remediation_v2.remediation: Dependency resolved: MT-081 is now implemented+hardened+verified (commit 5876feaf) and READY_FOR_VALIDATION. This MT own implementation passed adversarial review; the only V1 blocker was the MT-081 dependency. Resubmitting for V2 re-validation.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-093 requires re-index to mark moved/deleted knowledge sources stale without hard-deleting history or breaking citations, with EventLedger-receipted, idempotent stale marking against real PostgreSQL. The code (engine.rs:620-678 + storage/knowledge.rs:3624) implements exactly this: stale-only UPDATE (no DELETE), disposition moved/deleted with moved_to target, EventLedger receipt per mutation, proven by a real-PG lifecycle test. The prior FAIL_V1 was a dependency-block (MT-081 not yet validated), now resolved, not a code defect.
findings: engine.rs:620-678 — deleted/moved detection loop: compares surviving relative paths (seen_paths) against list_knowledge_sources_for_root; vanished content => disposition 'deleted', identical content_hash at a new path (different source_id) => 'moved' with moved_to target. Skips already-stale and NULL-relative_path (non-file) sources. Marks stale then appends an EventLedger source_stale_marked receipt with actor/session/correlation ids.

storage/knowledge.rs:3624 mark_knowledge_source_stale is a real-PG `UPDATE knowledge_sources SET stale=TRUE, updated_at=NOW() ... RETURNING`; grep confirms NO `DELETE FROM knowledge_sources` anywhere in engine.rs/store.rs — history is never hard-deleted, satisfying the 'without deleting history or breaking citations' requirement.
Sub-rule 1 PASS: grep of engine.rs/store.rs for todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/'not yet wired'/'deferred to follow-on'/'pending MT-'/Unavailable variants returns no hits on the required path.

Sub-rule 2 PASS: proof touches real Handshake-managed PostgreSQL. tests/knowledge_ingestion_support.rs:4-5 documents 'real Handshake-managed PostgreSQL, per-test isolated schema, full migration chain — no SQLite, no mocks'; mt093 test (knowledge_ingestion_lifecycle_tests.rs:127-246) runs two real ingestion passes, asserts moved/deleted dispositions, queries kernel_event_ledger directly (line 228-237) for the source_stale_marked event, confirms the old source row still exists with stale=true and non-empty extraction receipts (lines 211-225), and proves idempotence on pass 3 (line 245).
Sub-rule 3 PASS: claimed_by=KERNEL_BUILDER, validator verdict set by a distinct WP_VALIDATOR session; this independent review re-verified the proof is real, not self-certified.

Spec ALIGNED: spec module 02-system-architecture.md:2726 establishes authority is never hard-deleted ('deleting every compiled page MUST leave authority byte-identical') with first-class staleness verdicts; marking moved/deleted sources stale while retaining citable rows + receipts matches this.

Non-blocking nuance (already noted in adversarial review): moved-vs-deleted disposition uses content-hash equality (engine.rs:638-644), so two distinct files with identical bytes where one is deleted could be mislabeled 'moved'. This is a disposition-hint accuracy edge case only — the row is still correctly marked stale with no authority loss; not a fail.
Aside (non-blocking): the packet cites v02.192 anchors while current spec is v02.194 — expected paperwork drift, resolved against current spec, not a failing reason.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
